meta_title: Impact Doors in Lighthouse Point, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact doors in Lighthouse Point, FL. Protect your waterfront home with ESW & CWI doors. Free quotes, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors Built for Lighthouse Point's Waterfront Lifestyle
intro:
Lighthouse Point is one of Broward County's most coveted addresses — a tight-knit, waterfront community where canal-front estates and beautifully maintained homes average $750,000 in value and residents take serious pride in their properties. But living just one mile from the Atlantic Ocean in South Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone comes with a very real responsibility: making sure every point of entry into your home is engineered to withstand the fury of a major hurricane. That responsibility starts at your front door.

For homeowners along the waterways near Lighthouse Point Marina, the Cap's Place area, and throughout the Lighthouse Point Waterfront neighborhood, impact doors aren't a luxury upgrade — they're a critical layer of protection. Standard entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors simply cannot hold up against the 170 mph wind loads that Broward County's coastal communities are required to plan for. When hurricane-force winds strike an unprotected door, the result is catastrophic: sudden pressure changes blow out roofs, interiors flood in minutes, and structural integrity collapses rapidly. An impact-rated door rated for HVHZ conditions holds that wall of wind and debris at bay, keeping your family safe and your home intact.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — we specialize in providing Lighthouse Point homeowners with premium, code-compliant impact doors from industry-leading brands ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. With more than 60 years of combined installation experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our 100% in-house installation teams understand the unique demands of coastal South Florida construction. Whether you're protecting a classic mid-century canal home that predates modern building codes or upgrading a newly renovated waterfront estate, we bring the expertise, products, and service that Lighthouse Point deserves.

section_why.h2: Why Lighthouse Point Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
Not every contractor operating in South Florida has the depth of experience required to properly install impact doors in a High Velocity Hurricane Zone community like Lighthouse Point. HVHZ installations demand precision engineering, strict adherence to Florida Building Code protocols, and a thorough understanding of how coastal exposure, salt air, and extreme wind loads interact with your home's structure over time. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our reputation — and our A+ BBB rating — on doing this work the right way, every single time.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We never subcontract your project to unfamiliar crews. Every technician who arrives at your Lighthouse Point home is a trained, vetted member of our team who understands our quality standards inside and out. This means consistent craftsmanship from the first measurement to the final inspection. With over 500 homes successfully completed across South Florida, we have seen virtually every door configuration, framing challenge, and coastal construction scenario that Broward County can present.

Our process begins with a thorough on-site assessment of your home's existing door openings, frame conditions, and structural anchoring points. For the many Lighthouse Point homes built before 1994 — which account for roughly 55% of the city's housing stock — this evaluation is especially critical. Older door frames often require reinforcement or replacement before new impact units can be installed correctly and permitted successfully. Our team identifies these issues upfront so there are no surprises mid-project.

From the moment you contact us through final permit sign-off with the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step of your installation. We handle the permit applications, coordinate inspections, and ensure full compliance so you can focus on what matters most — enjoying your waterfront home with complete peace of mind. That's what being The Kings of Service means in Lighthouse Point.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Proves Why Impact Doors Are Non-Negotiable in Lighthouse Point
section_history.body:
Lighthouse Point has weathered more than 10 named storms since 1990, and the scars of that history are woven into the community's collective memory. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains a defining event for all of South Florida — a Category 5 monster that rewrote building codes and exposed just how vulnerable standard residential construction was to extreme wind events. While Andrew's worst damage concentrated in Miami-Dade County, its reach into Broward was undeniable, and the storm served as a brutal wake-up call for coastal communities like Lighthouse Point to rethink how they protected their homes.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 hit closer to home for Lighthouse Point residents. Crossing South Florida from the southwest, Wilma devastated Broward County with sustained winds and a massive storm surge that sent water pouring into waterfront neighborhoods. Homes without impact-rated doors and windows suffered significant interior flooding and structural damage, while properties with upgraded protection fared dramatically better. The contrast was stark and lasting. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017 — a storm that maintained intensity well into its Broward County passage, knocking out power to hundreds of thousands and demonstrating once again that coastal communities in South Florida are never truly out of hurricane season danger.

For a city where canal access and proximity to the Intracoastal Waterway are central to the lifestyle — and where homes near Lighthouse Point Marina and Cap's Place sit at the confluence of wind and water exposure — the lesson from decades of storms is clear: entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors are among the most vulnerable points on any structure when a hurricane strikes. Impact doors rated for 170 mph wind loads provide the frontline defense that Lighthouse Point's storm history demands.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Permit Requirements for Impact Doors in Lighthouse Point
section_code.body:
Lighthouse Point sits fully within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that triggers the most stringent residential construction and product standards in the entire state. HVHZ status is not merely a label — it carries force of law through the Florida Building Code, specifically Chapter 16 (Structural Design) and the Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) product approval system. Every impact door installed in Lighthouse Point must carry a valid NOA or Florida Product Approval (FPA) number demonstrating it has been tested and certified to withstand the wind pressures associated with a 170 mph design wind speed. No exceptions, no shortcuts.

For homeowners, this means working with a contractor who understands both the product approval system and the local permit process administered by the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division. Pulling the proper permits isn't just a legal requirement — it's a safeguard that ensures your installation is inspected by a licensed building official who verifies the work meets code. Unpermitted impact door installations not only expose homeowners to fines and forced removal but can also invalidate insurance claims at exactly the moment you need coverage most.

The permitting process in Lighthouse Point typically requires product approval documentation, a signed contract with a licensed contractor (like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing), and plans or product spec sheets identifying the door unit and its installation method. Our team assembles and submits all required documentation on your behalf and coordinates with the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division to schedule the required inspections. For homes built before Florida's post-Andrew building code revisions took effect, there may be additional structural considerations — our experienced crews identify and address these before installation begins, ensuring a smooth permit approval process from start to finish.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Lighthouse Point's Coastal Demands
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the industry's most respected manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for South Florida's demanding coastal environment, carrying the Miami-Dade and Florida Product Approval certifications required for HVHZ installation in Lighthouse Point. These aren't off-the-shelf doors with hurricane stickers — they are purpose-built systems designed and tested to maintain structural integrity under the kind of wind and impact loads that Broward County's coastline routinely threatens.

ESW impact entry doors are constructed with heavy-gauge aluminum frames reinforced at all critical stress points, combined with laminated glass or solid panel inserts that resist both wind pressure and windborne debris impact. Their multi-point locking systems engage simultaneously at multiple points along the door frame, distributing load forces evenly and dramatically reducing the risk of door failure during a storm. CWI offers a complementary lineup that includes beautifully designed impact sliding glass doors and French doors — perfect for Lighthouse Point's indoor-outdoor waterfront living style — engineered to meet the same exacting HVHZ standards without sacrificing aesthetic appeal.

For Lighthouse Point homeowners who entertain on their docks and patios near the marina or enjoy the open-air ambiance characteristic of canal-front living, CWI's sliding glass door systems provide expansive glass panels, smooth operation, and seamless hurricane protection. French door configurations in both the ESW and CWI lines allow for dramatic entryways that complement high-value waterfront home designs while meeting every code requirement mandated by the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division. Salt-air resistant finishes are standard across our product selection, ensuring long-term durability in the corrosive coastal atmosphere that defines life so close to the Atlantic.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Investment and Financing Options for Lighthouse Point Homeowners
section_cost.body:
In a community where the average home value reaches $750,000, investing in premium impact doors is one of the most financially intelligent home improvements a Lighthouse Point homeowner can make. The cost of impact door installation varies depending on the size, style, and number of openings being upgraded. A single impact entry door replacement typically ranges from $2,500 to $5,500 installed, while larger sliding glass door systems or multi-panel French door configurations can range from $4,000 to $12,000 or more depending on dimensions and specifications. For whole-home door packages — particularly in larger waterfront properties — budgeting $15,000 to $35,000 or more is not uncommon.

These numbers may seem significant, but context matters enormously. Consider that a single hurricane event causing interior flooding through a failed door can result in tens of thousands of dollars in remediation costs — and that's before accounting for the displacement and emotional toll of a major loss. Impact doors typically recoup 70% to 85% of their installation cost in increased resale value in high-value markets like Lighthouse Point, where buyers actively seek properties with full impact protection already in place.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these investments accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our financing programs are designed to let you protect your home today without waiting to save the full cost upfront. Combined with the insurance premium savings detailed below and the long-term durability of ESW and CWI products, impact doors represent a return on investment that compound year after year. Contact our team for a detailed, no-obligation quote tailored to your specific Lighthouse Point property.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Costs in Lighthouse Point
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact-rated doors in Lighthouse Point is the reduction in annual homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has been notoriously challenging in recent years, with many carriers restricting coverage or exiting the state entirely — but for homeowners who have invested in impact protection, the story is different. Insurance companies recognize that impact-rated openings dramatically reduce their risk exposure, and they reward that investment through wind mitigation credits that can reduce your annual premium by 20% to 45% depending on your current coverage and carrier.

The process begins with a wind mitigation inspection — a standardized evaluation conducted by a licensed inspector who verifies that your impact doors and windows meet the criteria established by Florida's wind mitigation form (OIR-B1-1802). When your inspector confirms that all openings are protected by HVHZ-rated impact products, the resulting report qualifies your home for the maximum opening protection credit available under your policy. For a Lighthouse Point homeowner paying $8,000 to $15,000 or more annually in homeowners insurance — not uncommon for high-value coastal properties — a 30% reduction represents savings of $2,400 to $4,500 every single year.

Major carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Insurance, all utilize the wind mitigation credit system. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installations come with all necessary product approval documentation to support your wind mitigation inspection, and our team is happy to connect you with licensed inspectors familiar with Lighthouse Point properties. Over a 10-year period, the cumulative insurance savings alone can offset a substantial portion — or in some cases, the entirety — of your impact door installation cost, making the upgrade essentially self-financing over time.

faqs.q: Do impact doors really make a difference for homes near Lighthouse Point Marina and the Intracoastal?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Waterfront and near-coastal properties in Lighthouse Point face amplified wind exposure and salt-air corrosion that accelerates the degradation of standard doors. Impact doors from ESW and CWI are engineered with salt-resistant finishes and tested to withstand 170 mph wind loads, protecting both your family and your investment. Properties with full impact door protection also suffer significantly less interior damage and insurance claims during storm events compared to homes with standard doors.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Lighthouse Point, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Lighthouse Point typically range from $2,500 to $5,500 for a single entry door and $4,000 to $12,000+ for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. When you factor in annual insurance savings of $2,000 to $4,500 and increased home resale value in Lighthouse Point's $750,000 average market, the return on investment is substantial.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door installation in Lighthouse Point, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All impact door installations in Lighthouse Point require permits from the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division. Because the city falls within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, all products must carry valid Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval certifications, and installations must be inspected by a licensed building official. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application process on your behalf, submitting all required documentation and coordinating inspections — you don't have to navigate city hall on your own.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a Lighthouse Point home?
faqs.a: Most single-door replacements can be completed in one day once permits are approved. Whole-home door packages or projects involving multiple large openings typically take two to three days of installation. Permit processing through the City of Lighthouse Point Building Division generally takes one to three weeks depending on current workload. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire timeline and communicates proactively so you always know where your project stands. Our 100% in-house crews mean no scheduling delays from subcontractors.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowners insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes — installing HVHZ-rated impact doors that protect all covered openings qualifies your Lighthouse Point home for wind mitigation credits on your homeowners insurance policy. A licensed wind mitigation inspector will verify the installation using Florida's standardized form (OIR-B1-1802), and the resulting report can reduce your annual premium by 20% to 45%. For high-value coastal properties in Lighthouse Point paying $8,000 to $15,000+ annually in insurance, those savings can amount to thousands of dollars every year — significantly offsetting the installation cost over time.
